title: Nashiak Llalik

{{Quote|My brother Nashiak is certainly the premier armor designer under contract with the Empire, aside from perhaps Dr. Nonrey, from whom we have not heard from in several years. He is not an evil mind; he has none of the cruel tendencies so often attributed to eccentric Imperial designs. He is just blind to the Empire's true evil: he believes that he is not responsible for how his inventions are used.|Doctor Saren Llalik, Alliance to Restore the Republic|<ref name="air" />}}
[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''Nashiak Llalik''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] born on [[Plavin 6]], was a prestigious [[Armor/Legends|armor]] designer. After graduating from the [[Chandrilan Academy of Sciences]], he was recruited by [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] and assigned to the [[Coalition for Progress/Legends|Coalition for Progress]]. Initially working for his own venture business, [[Llalik Designs]], he developed the coveted [[Sunder 9]] [[Power armor/Legends|power armor]]; however, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] laws prevented Llalik from continuing that line of work. Llalik then accepted contracts from the Empire to develop armor for [[Radiation zone assault trooper]]s, [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos]], and [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper]]s. Llalik was handsomely rewarded due to the quality of his work, and he used his fortune to buy a [[Nashiak Llalik's mansion|house]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. His younger sister, Doctor [[Saren Llalik]], an agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], thought that Nashiak was a potential recruit for the Alliance, because she believed he lacked any real [[Politics/Legends|political]] view.

==Biography==
Nashiak Llalik was born on [[Plavin 6]], a humid jungle [[Planet/Legends|world]]. He was the second of [[Doctor/Legends|Doctors]] [[Nash Llalik|Nash]] and [[Loran Llalik]]'s three children. During his childhood, Nashiak showed an interest in [[Armor/Legends|armor]] technology; he was fond of reading stories about the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] and [[Sun Guard]],<ref name="air" /> two notorious historical military groups that had used advanced armor.<ref name="tmtsb">''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> Llalik often entertained his younger sister, [[Saren Llalik|Saren]], by talking about these forms of armor. Later, Llalik studied at the [[Chandrilan Academy of Sciences]] and obtained a degree, graduating at the top of his class. Now a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], he was then recruited by the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] Science group, who in turn assigned him to the [[Coalition for Progress/Legends|Coalition for Progress]], both associated with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="air" />

For a long time, Llalik wanted to work on his pet project, the [[Sunder 9]] [[Power armor/Legends|power armor]]. He initiated a personal business venture, [[Llalik Designs]], which allowed him to work on the Sunder 9 for one year.<ref name="air" /><ref name="gft">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref> Llalik was his own test pilot for the Sunder 9 project, and as he was left-handed, the prototypes included some weapons in the left arm. However, new Imperial laws forbade most power armor,<ref name="air" /> and Llalik, having already built two untested prototypes, was unhappy.<ref name="air" /><ref name="gft" />

When Llalik was forced to leave the Sunder 9 project, the Empire hired him to work on armor for the [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper]].<ref name="air" /><ref name="gft" /> He had a chance to incorporate some concepts from the Sunder 9 in that project, although this was not enough to satisfy his own research interests. A talented designer, Llalik became responsible for the development of many Imperial and civilian armor suits, including the [[Radiation zone assault trooper]] armor and the [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commando]] armor. He also teamed up with [[Creshaldyne Industries/Legends|Creshaldyne Industries]] and [[Koromondain PDS, Incorporated]] in different designs. Thanks to the quality of his work, Llalik became a rich man.<ref name="air" />

He used the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] he earned to build a [[Nashiak Llalik's mansion|manor]] for himself on Plavin 6, even though he did not live on the planet any longer, hoping to return there one day in the future.<ref name="air" /> Llalik decided to store his Sunder 9 prototypes at his manor, and he set up [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]] to protect them. Several [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]] from throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] traveled to Plavin 6 wanting to acquire the Sunder 9 prototypes, but none of them managed to evade Llalik's security measures.<ref name="gft" />

Meanwhile, a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Task Force on Alliance Security|task force]] noticed that Llalik's designs were used to the Rebellion's detriment, and so it considered him to be a threat. However, Saren—who had since become a member of the Alliance—did not believe that Llalik was actually a [[Politics/Legends|political]] supporter of the Empire. She hoped that he could be recruited by the Rebellion not only to use his genius for a goal she deemed better, but also because she missed his stories about Sun Guard and Death Watch armor.<ref name="air" />

==Personality and traits==
Nashiak Llalik, a dark-skinned [[Human/Legends|Human]] with light green eyes, was fond of wearing loose clothes. He was qualified to test his own prototypes; and as he was left-handed, some of his test armor was customized for his particular use. Saren Llalik believed that Nashiak did not really share the political views of the Empire, as he had only started working toward Imperial interests when new laws stopped him from continuing to work on his own projects.<ref name="air" />

Llalik was an expert in the design and maintenance of armor, particularly power armor and certain specific designs, such as those used by the radtroopers, the [[Gravity/Legends|Zero-G]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], and the Storm commandos. He was also knowledgeable in other areas, including [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology. His other skills included the operation of armor and [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]], the use of armor-mounted weapons, and scholarly knowledge of historical armor and [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="air" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nashiak Llalik was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' ([[1994]]), published by [[West End Games]] and written by several authors. This book was a supplement to West End Games' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.
